summary: Matched another batch of visual/cutscene module functions across vi0401, vi0402, vi0501, vi1101, and vi1202, replacing several placeholder declarations and /// stubs with typed implementations. The PR added cutscene setup/animation/audio callbacks, improved public/static headers, introduced static headers for file-local data, and applied reviewer feedback in vi1202 by preferring typed arguments and M2C_FIELD-style field access for unresolved struct offsets. It was part 4 of 4 for visual/cutscene module functions and was reported to build and pass clang-format.
what_changed: Implemented vi0401 cutscene setup function un_8031D030 with a typed signature void un_8031D030(CharacterKind char_kind, int costume), initializing camera, collision, ground/stage, items, demo fighter allocation, player character/costume/id/slottype/facing/position, and audio state.
Added vi0401 animation callback fn_8031D168 and implemented un_8031D698_OnFrame to check gm_801A36A0(gm_8017E430()) for bit 0x1000, then call lb_800145F4 and gm_801A4B60 to exit/advance.

Implemented vi0402 model-loading helper un_8031D708, iterating un_804D6F68->models, creating GObjs, loading joints, setting GX links, applying animation, and registering un_8031D6E4 as the JObj animation proc.

Implemented vi0402 OnEnter un_8031D858_OnEnter to initialize audio/effects, load Vi0402.dat symbols for visual0402Scene, create light and camera GObjs, attach camera animation, run un_8031D708, trigger vi_8031C9B4(0x22, 0), initialize players, and restore audio state.

Implemented vi0501 camera callback fn_8031DD14 with frame-triggered vi_8031C9B4 and lbAudioAx_800237A8 calls based on static frame markers un_804DE074 through un_804DE088, ending on cobj animation end_frame.
Added vi0501.static.h to hold file-local static f32 frame markers un_804DE074, un_804DE078, un_804DE07C, un_804DE080, un_804DE084, and un_804DE088 plus required baselib includes.

Implemented vi1101 callbacks fn_8031F548, static fn_8031F56C, and fn_8031F600, covering JObj animation, camera erase/display behavior using un_804D5B08, shadow control, frame-triggered audio/visual events, and end_frame transition.
Extended vi1101.static.h with baselib includes and static f32 timing markers un_804DE0E4, un_804DE0E8, un_804DE0EC, and un_804DE0F0.
Implemented vi1202 un_80321294 as a typed HSD_GObj camera animation callback that triggers vi_8031C9B4(0xe, 0) at frame 1.0F and exits at end_frame.

In vi1202, added or refined matching code around un_80321900, un_80321950, fn_803219AC, un_80321A00, un_80321AF4, un_80321EBC, un_8032201C, un_80322314, un_803224DC, and un_80322598 according to the PR summary and diff excerpt.

Updated headers to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete signatures for matched functions, including un_8031D030, un_8031D708, un_8031D858_OnEnter, un_8031D9F8, fn_8031DD14, fn_8031F548, fn_8031F600, and un_80321294.
Adjusted vi1202 unresolved global/struct offset accesses to use M2C_FIELD in places such as Fighter_804D6500 offsets and mpLib_80458868 offsets, consistent with review feedback.
smart_moves: Split file-local static data into .static.h headers for vi0501 and vi1101, making frame marker globals available to the matching C file without exposing them as normal public API.
Converted placeholder function declarations to typed prototypes as functions were matched, reducing dependence on UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS and improving callsite checking.

Used existing engine helpers rather than open-coding behavior: GObj_Create, HSD_GObjObject_80390A70, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_CObjAnim, Player_* setup functions, lbAudioAx_* calls, and gm_* transition functions.
Preserved exact animation-frame event behavior by comparing cobj->aobj->curr_frame against known static f32 markers and end_frame, a recurring pattern for cutscene callback matches.
Responded to unresolved field-access concerns in vi1202 by using M2C_FIELD for raw offset accesses where the real field names/types were not yet available.
Kept private helper callbacks static when appropriate, as seen with vi0401_8031D18C/vi0401_8031D23C existing statics and newly static fn_8031F56C.
decomp_lessons: For cutscene modules, matching often requires reconstructing the whole scene pipeline: archive symbol load, lights, camera object, model GObjs, animation requests, proc registration, audio setup, and final scene transition checks.
Animation callbacks commonly use HSD_CObjAnim or HSD_JObjAnimAll followed by exact curr_frame comparisons to trigger visual effects, sound effects, and scene transitions.

When a function takes an HSD_GObj callback argument, changing the declaration from UNK_PARAMS to HSD_GObj* is both clearer and review-preferred once callsites and GET_COBJ/GET_JOBJ usage prove the type.
If m2c output produces raw pointer arithmetic into incompletely modeled globals or structs, either add the appropriate field to the type or use M2C_FIELD rather than hand-written casts.
Reviewer guidance specifically notes that m2c with --valid-syntax can generate M2C_FIELD usages when it cannot determine the proper field.
Public headers should expose only the matched external interface; internal static callbacks should not remain in public headers unless actually exported or needed.
File-local timing constants or BSS variables can be declared in module-specific static headers to support matching while keeping the public header clean.
The presence of explicit build and clang-format test-plan checks is useful for PR slices with many small signature/include changes.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions keep address-based names such as un_8031D030, un_8031D708, un_80321294, un_80321AF4, and un_803224DC.
Lifecycle callbacks use suffixes such as _OnEnter and _OnFrame, for example un_8031D858_OnEnter, un_803218E0_OnFrame, and un_8031D698_OnFrame.
Anonymous internal callbacks may use fn_ address names, such as fn_8031D168, fn_8031DD14, fn_8031F548, fn_8031F56C, fn_8031F600, and fn_803219AC.
Module-prefixed identified helpers use vi####_address style, such as vi0401_8031D18C, vi0401_8031D23C, vi_8031D80C, vi_8031DC80, and vi_8031E0F0_OnFrame.
Static/global data retains address-based names such as un_804DE074, un_804DE0E4, un_804D6F68, un_804D7050, and un_804D5B08.
Unknown structs use address/module-derived names such as un_804D6F58_t and vi1202_UnkStruct, with offset field names like x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, and x24 until better semantic names are known.
Per-module private static headers use the pattern vi0501.static.h and vi1101.static.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use typed baselib object access macros GET_JOBJ and GET_COBJ once a GObj callback’s object type is known.
Match HSD animation callbacks by preserving curr_frame and end_frame comparisons exactly, including float literals such as 1.0F and static f32 frame globals.
Use M2C_FIELD(base, type, offset) for unresolved struct/global offset reads instead of manual casts like *(f32*)((char*)base + offset), unless the relevant type can be extended with a proper field.
For m2c-assisted work, run m2c with --valid-syntax so unresolved field accesses are emitted as M2C_FIELD and can be regex-replaced or refined later.
Scene construction matches can be built from repeated GObj patterns: create GObj, attach HSD object, set GX link, request animation, run initial animation tick, then register a proc.
Header matching tactic: after proving parameter types from implementation and callsite usage, replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete return and argument types to stabilize future work.
When matching callbacks that end a cutscene, preserve the exact transition sequence lb_800145F4 followed by gm_801A4B60 where shown.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.lesson: Once a callback is known to receive HSD_GObj*, update the function signature instead of leaving a generic or void pointer form.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Access the proper field or use M2C_FIELD. If using m2c locally, pass --valid-syntax and it will generate M2C_FIELD usages when it cannot determine the proper field.
review_feedback.lesson: Prefer typed field access or M2C_FIELD over ad hoc pointer arithmetic for unknown fields.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Again, just use M2C_FIELD, or ideally, add the appropriate field to the relevant type. A lot of this can probably be regexed into M2C_FIELD usages.
review_feedback.lesson: For large blocks of raw offset accesses, systematically convert to M2C_FIELD or improve the underlying struct definitions.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that renamed vi1202_UnkStruct fields or replaced M2C_FIELD accesses in vi1202.c with proper struct fields.
Search for subsequent matches of un_8031D288_OnEnter, un_8031DE58_OnEnter, un_8031F294, and un_8031F714_OnEnter, which remained stubbed in this excerpt.
Check whether un_803224DC was later fixed to return a bool value on all control-flow paths after calling un_8032201C.
Compare other vi cutscene modules for the same HSD_CObjAnim curr_frame/end_frame audio-trigger pattern.
Search for additional .static.h introductions in vi modules to understand the project convention for file-local BSS/static data.
